items.text:
🎬 LOS ANGELES FILM CREW & PRODUCTION VOLUNTEERS NEEDED – JULY 31
We’re currently building our production team for a professionally filmed, broadcast-quality awards gala taking place on Friday, July 31 in Inglewood, CA.

This is an opportunity to gain hands-on experience on a high-caliber, multi-camera live production while supporting an incredible cause. The evening will feature celebrity honorees, VIP guests, live musical performances, and cinematic video elements, with proceeds benefiting scholarships and educational opportunities for underserved students and families throughout Los Angeles.

We’re looking for experienced professionals, recent graduates, and advanced college students interested in the following roles:

• Production Manager

• Stage Manager

• Assistant Stage Manager

• Video Director / Switcher Operator (experienced)

• Camera Operator

• Playback / Graphics Operator

• Audio Assistant

• Lighting Technician

• Event Photographer

• Social Media Content Creator

• VIP / Talent Coordinator

• Production Assistants / Runners

Crew Details

📍 Inglewood, CA

📅 Friday, July 31

🕒 Crew Call: Approximately 3:00 PM

💻 One mandatory virtual production meeting prior to the event (scheduled around crew availability)

What You’ll Receive

✅ Professional production credit for your résumé/portfolio

✅ Hands-on experience on a large-scale live event

✅ Networking with experienced production and event professionals

✅ Meals provided

✅ Parking provided

Limited stipends may be available for select specialized technical positions based on experience.

If you’re interested, please email tdninvestmentgroups@gmail.com with:

* Your résumé

* A brief summary of your experience

* Production credits and/or portfolio (if available)

* The position(s) you’re interested in

* Your contact information

We’re looking for passionate, dependable team members who want to contribute to a meaningful event while gaining valuable live production experience. We look forward to hearing from you!

Angela Boehm Casting is now casting a FEMALE STAND-IN for the Feature Film 'DRIFTER.' 

This project will be taking place in the Northeast, OH area.

Please read carefully below for details.

DETAILS:

Project: Feature Film 'DRIFTER'

Filming Location: Northeast, OH area

Shoot Dates:  Approximately 8 days during the shoot spread of July 15th through July 24th 2026.

*These dates are subject to change, so please only submit if you are FULLY AVAILABLE for ALL DATES with flexibility.*

Stand-In Rate: $128 for 8 hours

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Stand-In

Gender: Female

Ethnicity: White

Age: 18+

Height: 5'5" - 5'7"

Weight: 100lbs - 120lbs

HOW TO SUBMIT:
In order to submit for this role, please send an email to team@angelaboehmcasting.com with the following information:

SUBJECT LINE: DRIFTER Female Stand-In - (YOUR FULL NAME)

PLEASE INCLUDE:

- Full Name

- Height

- Weight

- Phone Number

- 1 CURRENT Waist-Up or Headshot Photo

- 1 CURRENT Full-Body Photo

- Let us know of any past film or stand-in experience.

- Any conflicts on/around the shoot spread of July 15th through July 24th 2026.

***PLEASE REMEMBER: These dates are tentative and subject to change, so please only submit if you are FULLY AVAILABLE for ALL DATES with flexibility.***

PLEASE NOTE: This is just a submission. If you are booked, we will reach out to YOU.

Kind regards,
Angela Boehm Casting

Angela Boehm Casting is now casting paid Background EXTRAS for an upcoming film "Raleigh VS" filming in the WOOSTER, OH area for TWO DAYS on Friday, July 10th 2026 & Friday, July 24th 2026. (Talent will have the opportunity to work both dates, but will not be required to be considered for this project.)

*THIS WILL BE A PAID OPPORTUNITY!!*

This project will be taking place in the WOOSTER, OH Area (please don't submit if you are unable to work locally in Wooster).
Please read carefully below for details!

DETAILS:
Project: 'Raleigh VS'

Filming Location: WOOSTER, OH Area (MUST WORK LOCAL)

Shoot Date(s): Fri. July 10th, 2026 & Fri. July 24th, 2026

Pay rate: $75.00 FLAT RATE for 8 hours
(we understand this is a lower-paying job. This is a smaller film, though, and Production STILL wanted to make sure their Extras were paid, so we want to help send them great extras. If you want to get on set, network, and be part of a period feature film, feel free to continue with the form below!)

CLICK HERE TO SUBMIT: https://bit.ly/RVSABC

Angela Boehm Casting Team

YOUTH OF FREE STATE 

This is your chance ! 

Register Now ! Not tomorrow or waiting for last minutes ! 

For more informations and Updates ! Remember to follow this page Re Phethile Art Production

sharedPost.text:
RE PHEITHILE ART PRODUCTION IS EXPANDING
 
“Where There Is No Art, A People Die Inside.” 

NEW BRANCHES NOW OPEN IN YOUR TOWN!

Young Creatives of the Free State — This is YOUR moment!

Re Phethile Art Production is growing. We are bringing art, culture & opportunity out of the cities and into your kasi, your school, your community. 

“Art is not a luxury. It is the oxygen of the soul.”

We are now officially recruiting in:
  
✨️Bethlehem
✨️Qwaqwa
✨️Paul Roux
✨️Reitz
✨️Vrede
✨️Senekal
✨️Clarens
✨️Fouriesburg
✨️Rosendal
✨️Lindley  
✨️Kestel
✨️Botshabelo
✨️Petrus Steyn
✨️Brandfort

Additional branches will be announced soon. Youth in other towns, contact us directly to request a branch in your area.

If you live for:
💃🕺Dance
 🎭 Drama
 🎶  Music
🎬 Film  
✍️ Storytelling
🎙Poetry 
🌍 Culture   
📝🖌✏️ Visual Arts
🪘👏 Gwijo & Acappella 

Then build with us.Let’s bring the kasi to life and revive Art & Culture where it matters most. 

“A nation that does not tell its own stories will be told by others.”

EVERYONE IS WELCOME 

All races, all backgrounds — Black, Coloured, Indian, and White youth. Art has no boundaries, only possibilities.

---
HOW TO JOIN:

WhatsApp “JOIN” to 081 750 9746 with:  
1.  Name & Surname  
2.  Age & Gender 
3.  Town 
4.  Recent Picture 

Be part of the movement. Be part of the legacy.
  
Please SHARE this with every young creative you know ✨ 

#RePhethileArt #FreeStateCreativeEconomy #ArtRevival #YouthInArts #KasiToTheWorld

Reviving the Kasi: Why Re Phethile Matters Now” 

There was a time when every kasi had a theatre group, a dance crew practicing on dusty tar, and a choir that could make your chest vibrate. Culture was not something we watched on TV. It was something we lived. 

Today, many of our young people have talent, but they do not have a stage. They have stories, but no microphone. They have dreams, but no structure. That is why Re Phethile Art Production is expanding.

Re Phethile means “we have done it” in Sesotho. It is a declaration. It is a promise that we will not wait for opportunity to come to us. We will build it, town by town, branch by branch. 

From Bethlehem to Botshabelo, Qwaqwa to Clarens, Reitz to Brandfort — we are opening doors. Not just for dancers, actors, or musicians. But for every young person who believes that art can change a life, and a community. 

“If you can dream it, you can stage it.”This is the heartbeat of our work. We believe that poetry can heal, drama can teach, dance can unite, and film can document our truth for generations. 

We welcome every race, every language, every background. Because art does not ask where you come from. It asks what you are willing to create. 

To the youth of the Free State: This is your moment. Do not let it pass you by. WhatsApp us. Show up. Bring your voice, your body, your ideas. We will do the rest together. 

Be part of the movement. Be part of the legacy. 

A re tsoeleng pele ka bonono le setso. Forward with Art & Culture.

items.text:
CASTING CALL – THE AFTER (Short Film)
Logline

A woman numbed by grief after her mother’s death finds comfort in the unexpected return of a childhood friend, until the true reason for the visit forces them to confront how far they’ve drifted apart—and just how much life has changed.

Synopsis

The After follows Klara, whose life has ground to a halt after losing her mother. Struggling with overwhelming grief and the financial burden left behind by years of caring for her, she finds herself isolated and unable to move forward.

The unexpected arrival of her childhood best friend, June, offers a brief escape from the weight of loss. As the two reconnect, old memories resurface and long-buried tensions begin to emerge. What starts as a heartfelt reunion gradually reveals itself to be something far more complicated, forcing both women to confront the lives they’ve built apart and the people they’ve become.

The film explores the quiet violence of stagnation. Rather than presenting grief as a single event, it examines the slow accumulation of ordinary moments that follow loss: unpaid bills, empty cupboards, unanswered messages, and the exhausting performance of convincing others that everything is fine. Alongside this sits June's experience of emotional suffocation within privilege-a different but very real kind of paralysis, hidden beneath comfort and expectation. Together, their stories ask how we continue living when identity has quietly slipped away.

We are currently casting the following supporting roles:

DANA WILLIAMS (Male, Early 30s) – Supporting

Dana Williams is Klara’s long-time friend, former

colleague and co-owner of their company. He’s married to a woman named Emma whom he met during his senior year at university.

At work, he and Klara always joked that they were each one half of a brain, and together they made one whole brain that runs the company. He’s incredibly intelligent and creative when it comes to coming up with solutions, and is the kinder of the two. His feelings for Klara started back in university, but when she made it clear that they were just friends he backed off and met Emma soon there after. When Klara left the company to care for her mother, he realizes that he is still in love with her. When he notices that she is struggling to support her mother, he quickly jumps in to aid her, hoping, secretly, that she might grow to love him back

MATTHEW DOVEY (Male, 31) – Supporting

Matthew Dovey (31) is June’s fiancé. He works as an Econ professor at the University of Portland. He’s been with June for the past 6 years and is ready to settle down and start a life with her. He proposed to her on Valentine’s Day, and they’re set to get married in October of that same year, since his mother had been preparing for their wedding for years.

He loves June deeply, but doesn’t seem to fully

understand the friction between her and his family, so

when she suddenly runs away to England, barely a month before they’re supposed to get married, it takes him a while to realise it might have been something he/his family has said or done that eventually pushed her away.

💵 Rate: £200 per day

🎬 Filming: Mid & South Wales

📅 Dates: October 2026 (provisional)

🏡 Accommodation: Available where required

🍔Catering: Provided on set

🌎 Casting: Worldwide

To apply, please email theafterfilm2026@gmail.com with your Spotlight or Backstage profile.

If you are not represented on either platform, please include recent headshots, a showreel (where available), and your CV or acting credits.

Shortlisted actors will be invited to submit a self-tape before meeting with the director and production team in person.

Due to the volume of submissions, we’re only able to consider actors who closely match the character descriptions.

We’d greatly appreciate shares to help us reach as many suitable actors as possible.

Thank you—we look forward to hearing from you.

The After Film Team

Letzte Chance dabei zu sein!
Offenes Jurycasting im Kloster Graefenthal in Goch

Die Suche nach neuen Talenten geht weiter!

Du begeisterst mit deiner Stimme, deiner Tanzperformance, Artistik, Comedy oder einem anderen außergewöhnlichen Talent? 

Dann nutze jetzt die Gelegenheit und präsentiere dein Können unserer Jury. 

Ganz gleich, ob Anfänger oder Bühnenerfahrung wir freuen uns auf alle Talente.

Jetzt: Sonntag, 12. Juli 2026

12:00 bis 18:00 Uhr

Kloster Graefenthal

Maasstraße 48–50

47574 Goch

Bitte bringe Folgendes mit:

• Gute Laune und Freude an deinem Auftritt

• Bei Gesang, Tanz oder Comedy: einen USB-Stick mit deinem Playback

• Musiker und Bands: eigene Instrumente, Verstärker und benötigte Anschlüsse

• Artisten und Akrobaten: alle für den Auftritt erforderlichen Requisiten

• Minderjährige können nur in Begleitung eines Erziehungsberechtigten teilnehmen.

Spontan teilnehmen? Kein Problem!

Eine vorherige Anmeldung ist nicht erforderlich. Du kannst einfach vorbeikommen und dich direkt vor Ort für das Jurycasting anmelden.

Wer sich seinen Platz bereits im Voraus sichern möchte, kann sich bequem online registrieren.

Online-Bewerbung & weitere Informationen:

www.evctalentcastingshow.de/bewerbung

Kennst du jemanden mit einem besonderen Talent?

Dann markiere die Person in den Kommentaren oder teile diesen Beitrag. 

Vielleicht beginnt genau an diesem Sonntag der Weg bis ins große Finale der EVC Talent Casting Show 2026.

Wir freuen uns auf einen abwechslungsreichen Casting-Tag und viele beeindruckende Talente!

items.text:
Looking for 5 actors who can sing local to LA to be part of an Audio book illustration project for an original musical. The musical style is Jazz Hip Hop.Please note: THE PROJECT IS UNPAID
Please email headshot, resume, and a sample of your singing and/reel to Georgejosephr11@gmail.com.

DeAndre Westwood: 
Age: mid 30s early forties 
Ethnicity: Black
Occupation: Vice Principal of Diamond Hill High School
Description: Alumni of Diamond Hill High School. Very passionate about education and the personal responsibility of the individual. Although he recognizes peoples’ struggles do exist, he’s doesn’t personalize them.

Marcus Harrison:
Age: 40s
Ethnicity: Black
Occupation: Principal of Diamond Hill High school
Description: Harrison has been principal of Diamond Hill High School for the past 6 years. He was never a resident of the city but experienced similar struggles growing up in Harlem, New York. An individual who doesn’t mind getting his hands dirty believing he’s making the best possible decision for himself and everyone else when even the school board doesn’t care.

David Garner:
Age: 18
Occupation: Senior in High School
Ethnicity: Ambiguous
Description: David is a strong willed individual, jokester, but stands his ground. Raised by a single father he does his best to cope with the environment and circumstances he was dropped into.

Mr. Carpe/Robert Atkins
Age: 50s – 60s
Occupation: Janitor
Ethnicity: Black/African descent
Description: Mr. Carpe is a former teacher of Diamond Hill High from when Westwood was a student. He does this for the students' sake no longer needing to work but still wanting to do something for the next generation. He talks to Westwood and knows everything going on in the campus as the janitor but avoids getting involved to allow himself to present an unbiased standpoint for all sides

Jonathan Hernandez: 
Age: 16
Ethnicity: Hispanic/Latin descent
Occupation:  Senior in High School & Student Representative of the board (Dreamer Student)
Description: Jonathan has always had his decisions made for him. His parents baby him a lot because he’s slightly on the spectrum. He respects Jordan a lot because he doesn’t cut him any slack and treats him as fully capable. Jonathan is a smart kid full of potential who really likes going to school. Although he struggles to express himself he means well always putting the masses before himself.

Ethnicity: Black
Occupation: Vice Principal of Diamond Hill High School
Description: Alumni of Diamond Hill High School. Very passionate about education and the personal responsibility of the individual. Although he recognizes peoples’ struggles do exist, he’s doesn’t personalize them.

Marcus Harrison:
Age: 40s
Ethnicity: Black
Occupation: Principal of Diamond Hill High school
Description: Harrison has been principal of Diamond Hill High School for the past 6 years. He was never a resident of the city but experienced similar struggles growing up in Harlem, New York. An individual who doesn’t mind getting his hands dirty believing he’s making the best possible decision for himself and everyone else when even the school board doesn’t care.

David Garner:
Age: 18
Occupation: Senior in High School
Ethnicity: Ambiguous
Description: David is a strong willed individual, jokester, but stands his ground. Raised by a single father he does his best to cope with the environment and circumstances he was dropped into.

Mr. Carpe/Robert Atkins
Age: 50s – 60s
Occupation: Janitor
Ethnicity: Black/African descent
Description: Mr. Carpe is a former teacher of Diamond Hill High from when Westwood was a student. He does this for the students' sake no longer needing to work but still wanting to do something for the next generation. He talks to Westwood and knows everything going on in the campus as the janitor but avoids getting involved to allow himself to present an unbiased standpoint for all sides

Jonathan Hernandez: 
Age: 16
Ethnicity: Hispanic/Latin descent
Occupation:  Senior in High School & Student Representative of the board (Dreamer Student)
Description: Jonathan has always had his decisions made for him. His parents baby him a lot because he’s slightly on the spectrum. He respects Jordan a lot because he doesn’t cut him any slack and treats him as fully capable. Jonathan is a smart kid full of potential who really likes going to school. Although he struggles to express himself he means well always putting the masses before himself.

items.text:
CASTING CALL - SHORT HORROR FILM (Pennsylvania)
Hey all!
My name is Colby Killian and I am a senior from Lycoming College. For my thesis film, I aim to create a roughly twenty-minute long narrative psychological horror short that will premiere at a festival on Lycoming College's campus this coming fall.
Synopsis: Radius of a Soul follows June Seymour, a young woman who frequently suffers dissociative thoughts relating to the mysterious disappearance of her closest friend, Nova Chalmers. To commemorate Nova on the anniversary of her disappearance, June and her friends, Max and Larry, have planned to share memories over dinner. Although they have all burdened this loss, June has experienced the most turmoil with her grief which Max and Larry refuse to understand. When the dinner is cut short due to disagreement, June has to decide whether to follow the haunting visions of Nova or believe the friends that don’t support her.
Currently, I am audition for all roles, most of which are in the 22-30 age range. Character breakdowns and age specifications below.
June Seymour- (woman age range 22-28 years old ~ 29 speaking lines) A young woman who lives alone despite her need for codependency. She struggles deeply with the disappearance of her best friend Nova Chalmers, believing Nova was the only one truly capable of understanding and supporting her. This has left her obsessive and desperate for similar connections, but her life only gets increasingly frustrating when she cannot find that. She has intrinsically tied herself to Nova, leaving her spiraling down a path of self-destruction.
Zircus- (man age range 25-50 years old ~ no speaking lines) A supernatural, animalistic life form with mysterious motives. Zircus originally existed only as a make-believe creature both June and Nova created when they were kids, but now has materialized into an entity beyond horror. Zircus has a strange attraction to June; the torment it brings her, the more its hold on her tightens.
(Note: This role will require the use of cosmetics. Please be aware of this if interested.)
Larry Holland- (man age range 22-28 years old ~ 33 speaking lines) June’s hotheaded and unemployed friend whose only concern in any situation is how it will benefit him first and foremost. Larry is wrapped up in his own careless lifestyle, but his immaturity and unwillingness to see things from a different perspective only serve to further draw a wedge in his and June’s relationship.
Maxwell Evans- (man age range 22-28 years old ~ 11 speaking lines) Another of June’s friends who oftentimes finds himself acting as a mediator between Larry and June, though keeping a front of indifference as to not further escalate conflict. As a family man, he gives all of his time and attention to his wife and child, frequently leaving himself emotionally exhausted and unenthusiastic to be there for June.
Nova Chalmers- (woman age range 22-28 years old ~ 4 speaking lines) June’s closest friend and supporter, whose disappearance haunts every aspect of June’s everyday life. Since vanishing, Nova’s likeness has been reduced to bait for June to unwittingly follow, luring her further into Zircus’s trap.
Filming is currently planned to take place sometime in mid-late July, primarily on weekends. Additionally, our main shooting locations are all located in Williamsport, Pennsylvania, I am very flexible and am open to discussions pertaining the schedule, transportation, etc. Lastly, this will be paid positions. I will get a clearer estimate on what pay will be as I continue to structure the budget.
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out at kilcolb@lycoming.edu. Alternatively, you may also reach me at (607)-358-0854.
Thank you for your time!
#auditions #castingcall #shortfilm #film #Pennsylvania

items.text:
📣 **Parents, considering home education from September? **
***Feeling nervous? Not sure where to start?***

Hi 👋🏼
We are Josh & Aimee, owners of Swan Tutoring! An online, flexible education platform for home educating families 🏡

In August** we are holding an **✨** Open Week **✨ designed for those who are considering home education and are new to it, and those who have been home educating for a while!** You can try our engaging online classes for just £3 (+VAT) per lesson per screen!**

☕️💬 There are also some FREE online **social events** included, giving you chance to meet other home educating families**.**

And receive a FREE **welcome pack** delivered to your door! 📦🏡

Let me know if you'd like more information! 😊
Best regards,
Aimee
Swan Tutoring

items.text:
Keeping the wheels moving, sometimes by sheer willpower alone, is part of their job description. 
The rest is on a need-to-know basis. 
Today we would like to introduce you to two more members of the Dark Theatre Team. 

🩸 Dracula: A Feminist Revenge Fantasy, Really by Kate Hamill
📅 29–31 October 2026
📍 St Aidan's Hall, Rochdale
🎟️ Tickets available soon

#DarkTheatreRochdale #Dracula #FeministTheatre #UKAmateurPremiere #MeetTheTeam

sharedPost.text:
Keeping the wheels moving, sometimes by sheer willpower alone, is part of their job description. 
The rest is on a need-to-know basis. 
Today we would like to introduce you to two more members of the Dark Theatre Team. 

🧥”I was once headbutted by a pigeon, and I have the scar to prove it. The injury may have been a contributing factor to the time I hid in a rack of coats in M&S and when anyone passed by I yelled,  “pick me!! Pick me!” ...”

🩷 We were over the moon when Andrea Chadderton-Mulrooney agreed to join us. Absolutely made for the role of Welfare & Safeguarding Manager, her encounters with feathered hooligans have given her a solid foundation for caring for our cast of avian-traumatised actors.
Andrea brings a unique gift to the Company. Her personality, kindness, and humour create a warm and safe environment for the cast to explore the places that the text asks them to go, without judgement.

🦇 Nature's Original Vampire?
Flamingo parents feed their young a bright, blood-red liquid. Containing actual blood cells, it is a rich "crop milk" packed with nutrients from their diet. The process is so exhausting it literally drains the color from the parents, turning them a ghostly white. 
Basically, nature’s original vampires are tall, pink, and absolutely exhausted. 🦩

As one of the very first people introduced to the ideas behind the show, Laura Healey was gently but consistently prodded into joining the team, with the irresistible promise of great techno beats. 
She has subsequently morphed into the hybrid role of Choreographer/Movement Lead/Physicality Designer, manifesting the dark, kinetic language for this production. 
Along with finding the frequency and pulse for our cast, we hope she can also create a job title that requires a little less spatial awareness from our programme designer!

🛌“I HAVE to touch my mattress (palms down) before I get in bed. It’s almost a welcoming ritual!”

🎭 About Laura 
Studying drama and performing arts through school, college and higher education, Laura finally pursued her dream and became an ISTD qualified dance teacher in 2011. She is also a qualified fitness instructor and personal trainer and has successfully run her own business in that field for the last 14 years. 
Laura has been lighting up amateur theatre since 1989 and has passed that passion down to her two beautiful children, who have shared a stage with her. While she pours her decades of movement expertise into choreography, she still loves a sneaky cameo appearance. In fact, she fully embraced our company's bizarre bird theme by recently playing a masked Vulture in Jack and the Beanstalk with utter conviction!

🦇 On being part of the Dracula team
“I am incredibly excited to be working on this production, the expression of movement mixed with the pure darkness of this show sends my creative head into a spiral…. Who knows where this will take us!”

🐺 The Geometry of the Hunt?
When a wolf pack moves through a forest, they communicate entirely through micro-movements, spatial awareness, and non-verbal cues. They don't just run together; they shift lanes, adjust their pacing to match the weakest link, and use a collective physical rhythm to trap their target without making a single sound. Nature's most lethal hunters survive by locked-in choreography, perfect spacing, and a shared predatory pulse. Something the cast will definitely need to develop quickly through the rehearsal process 😉

🩸 Dracula: A Feminist Revenge Fantasy, Really by Kate Hamill
📅 29–31 October 2026
📍 St Aidan's Hall, Rochdale
🎟️ Tickets available soon

#DarkTheatreRochdale #Dracula #FeministTheatre #UKAmateurPremiere #MeetTheTeam
